I ANNEXUTCE THE BOMBAY HOME GUARDS ACT, 1947 AS EXTENDED TO THE UNION i i i e , o u m o TERRITORY OF TRIPURA BOMBAY ACT N O . Ill OF 1947 An Act to provide for the Constitution of Horns Guards. Whereas it is expedient to provide a volunteer organisation for use in emergen- c i e s r £ p for other purposes in the State of Bombay. It is hereby enacted as follows:— 1 Short title, extent a n a commencement.—(1) This Act may be called the Bombay Home Guards Act-, 1947. ' (2) It extends to the whole of the Union territory of Tripura. (3) It shall come into force at once. ** 1A. Definitions—In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires,— (a) "Chief Commissioner" means the Chief Commissioner of the Union territory of Tripura; (b) "Official Gazette" means the Tripura Gazette. 3 roHstitmioii of Home Guards anfl appointment of Commandant General anS Commandant—(1) The Chief Commissioner shall constitute for the Umon terri- t o r T o f Tripura a volunteer body called the Home Guards, the members of whicn shall discharge such functions and duties in relation to t h e protection of Persons, the security of property and the public safety as may be assigned to them in accordance w i t h ' t h e provisions of this Act and the rules m a d e thereunder: Provided t h a t t h e Chief Commissioner may, by notification in t h e Official Gazette, divide t h e Union territory of T r i p u r a into two or more areas and con- stitute such a volunteer body for each such area. (1A) « (2) The Chief Commissioner may appoint a Commandant of each of the Home Guards constituted under sub-section ( 1 ) . (3) The Chtef Commissioner shall appoint a Commandant General oi[, the Horn- Guards in whom shaU vest the general supervision and control o± t&B H o S I Guards throughout the Union territory of Tripura and u n t i l j Coinnnand.nv is atasriSnted "nde?- sub-section f21 t h e Commandant General may also exercise fe/Swert a i d p e r f ^ m t h e functions assigned to the Commandant b y or under 'his Act. 3. a p p o i n t i n g (rf mn&mr^m Subject to the approval of the C o m m a n d ^ General the Commandant may appoint as members of the Home Guards a g f i a f t e r of uerses -- a r e fit and willing to serve, as may from -ime i0 time -- 2 of 5 -- A 1 such member to ijy post ^ ^ " ' i m m ^ t ^ b S r ™ 1 ™ 8 0 " ' ' , W , o i m a n * W ** ** &£ functions o r duties assigned t o t h p C c r , , , , " S t o discharge any of t h e sions of this Act Iff&iS^Sj^S.SSSt m 3 C C 0 r d a n c e w i t h * * * « * in *&J* ira&Sg* s£a11 b e i n s t i t " t e d against a member of t h e Home G u a r d s in respect of anything done or purporting to b e done b y h i m i n t h e discharge 6 Control by officers of police force.—The members of t h e Home Guards when S n S Tffl « n d e i ; ^ t i o a 4 i n aid of tire police force shall be S e r X e confrol of t h e officers of t h e police force in such m a n n e r and to such e x t e n t as m a v be prescribed b y rules made u n d e r section 8. e x t e n t aa m a y m ^ F v p f ^ S 0 ' * S 1 C " io b e W M « »P b ? K s tonedSte c o S r o L * -- 3 of 5 -- (2) When the C o m m a n d a n t General or the C o m m a n d a n t passes an order for suspending, reducing, dismissing or fining any member of the Home' Guards under sub-section (1), he shall record such order or cause the same to be recorded, together with the. reasons therefor and a note of the inquiry made, in writing, and no such order shall b e passed by the C o m m a n d a n t General or the Commandant unless t h e person concerned is given- a n opportunity to be heard in his defence. (3) Any member of the Home Guards aggrieved by a n order of the Com- m a n d a n t may- appeal against such order to the Commandant General and any such member aggrieved by an order of t h e Commandant General may appeal against such order to the. Chief Commissioner, within thirty days of the date on which he was served with notice of such order. The Commandant General or the Chief Commissioner, as the case may be, may pass such order as he thinks lit. (4) The Commandant General or the Chief Commissioner m a y at any time call for and examine the record of any order passed by the Commandant or Commandant General, respectively, under sub-section (1) or (1A) for the pur- pose of satisfying himself as to the legality or propriety of such order passed by the Commandant or the Commandant General, as the case may be, and may pass such order with reference thereto as he thinks fit. t5) Every order if no appeal is m a d e therefrom as hereinbefore provided a n d every order passed in appeal or revision under this section shall b e final, (6) Any fine imposed under this section may be recovered in the. manner provided by the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1893 (V of 189S), for the recovery of fines imposed by a Court as if such fine were imposed by a Court. (7) Any punishment inflicted on a member of the Home G u a r d s under this section shall be in addition to the penalty to which such m e m b e r is liable under section 7 or • any other law for the time being in force. Explanation.—Where the Commandant General, while exercising the powers ef the Commandant, passes any order under sub-section (1) or sub-section CIA)— (a) the appeal from such order shall lie to the Chief Commissioner;^ (b) for the purposes of sub-section (4), t h e power of revision in respect of such order shall vest in the Chief Commissioner. 7 Penalty. (1) If any member of the Home GuaTds, on being called out under section 4, without reasonable excuse neglects or refuses to obey such order, or to discharge Ins functions a s a m e m b e r of t h e Home Guards, or_ to obey any lawful order or direction given to h i m for the performance of his duties, h e shall on conviction, b e punishable w i t h simple imprisonment for a t e r m which may extend to t h r e e m o n t h s or with fine1 which may extend to two hundred and fifty rupees or with both. (1A) If any member of t h e Home Guards wilfully neglects or refuses to deliver u p his certificate of appointment or of office or any other article, in accordance with the provisions of sub-section (1) ot section 6A, he shall on conviction, be punished with imprisonment; for a term wmch may extend to „„„ m r t n+h m uri+fc fine whir-h mav extend to one Hundred rupees or witn DO in. one m o n t h or w i t h fine which may extend to one h u n d r e d rupees (2) No proceedings shall be instituted under sub-section (1) or (1A) with- out the previous sanction of the Commandant. (3) A Police officer may a r r e s t w i t h o u t warrant any• person who commits s n offence punishable under sub-section (1) or U A ) . 3. g a l e s — T h e Chief Commissioner may make rules consistent with this Act,— (a) providing for t h e exercise b y any officer of the H o m e Guards of file powers conferred by section 4 on the C o m m a n d a n t a n d the Com- m a n d a n t General; (b) providing for the exercise of control by officers of tha police fores over members of (.he Home Guards when acting in aid of the police force; f o regulating the organisation, appointment, conditions of service, func- tions I'US'Cinline, arms, accoutrements and d o m i n g of m e m b e r s Oi the Home Guards and the manner in which they m a y be called out for service; -- 4 of 5 -- <d) regulating the exercise by members of the Home £hm**e , r the powers exercisable under section 5 of thU Ac£ "* # Ce) generally for giving effect to the provisions of this *Act meaning of sfction 21 ofthe Indian & ?
